name: InstanceObserver
summary: This class is abstract. To implement this observer, create a Ruby class of this type, override the desired methods, and add an instance of the observer to the objects of interests. This observer interface is implemented to react to component instance events. Note that you may also attach InstanceObservers to Groups.

methods.name: onClose
methods.summary: The #onClose method is called when an instance is “closed,” meaning an end user was editing a component's geometry and then exited back into the parent's editing space.
methods.remarks: The #onClose method is called when an instance is “closed,” meaning an end user was editing a component's geometry and then exited back into the parent's editing space.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance that was just closed

methods.name: onOpen
methods.summary: The #onOpen method is called when an instance is “opened,” meaning an end user has double clicked on it to edit its geometry.
methods.remarks: The #onOpen method is called when an instance is “opened,” meaning an end user has double clicked on it to edit its geometry. This is particularly useful if your plugin is dynamically drawing geometry or performing transformations in global space, since when in “edit component” mode all transformations and positions are returned in relation to the current component's origin. This method will tell you when a user has entered edit mode, and you can then use Model#active_path and Model#edit_transform methods to determine any corrections you need to make to your transformations.
